p.seg: Let present and future know that this agreement between William de Brus, lord, and William de Heineville, that is, which I, William de Brus ... to William de Heineville and his heirs that land which is towards Logan above Blakebec and that land which is between Aybeg bec and Logan just as Aybegbec falls into the Serk, and ... the valley ... Serk ... as far as the Raeburn, and from the side ... from Serke as the boundaries ... so as to please him I grant to him and his heirs those ... ... of my father and my brother Robert de Brus; I also grant that land between Blabeok ... ... of my father and my brother ... ... also, I grant to him ... ... pasture in wood, in meadow ... ... he remits in common pasture to me those lands which were ploughed and settled from new just as the lands in which he had no right unless by my mandate. I also will and enjoin that my men have common right of the wood and of the pasture between him and them, just as they had in the time of my father.
